content.full_text:
November 6, 1987


Debbie Ortega
County of Tuolumne
Elections Division
2 South Green Street
Sonora, CA 95370

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance; Our File No. I-87-257

Dear Ms. Ortega:

You have requested our assistance regarding
conflict-of-interest complaints your office has received
regarding Ron Boyd-Snee, who is a director of the Tuolumne
Regional Water District and an employee of the James-
Sanitary District. Mr. Boyd-Snee has not authorized your
request; therefore, we are unable to provide specific guidance
to you about Mr. Boyd-Snee's duties under the Political Reform
Act.1/ (See Regulation 18329(b)(8), copy enclosed.) However,
to assist you with these questions we have enclosed a pamphlet
which summarizes the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act.

Please inform Mr. Boyd-Snee that he or his authorized
representative may request advice from this office concerning
his ability to participate in any pending governmental
decisions. The local county counsel or the legal counsel for
the water or sanitary districts also could assist Mr. Boyd-Snee
with these questions.

Complaints alleging that Mr. Boyd-Snee's past conduct has
violated the Act should be referred to our Enforcement
Division. Enclosed is a copy of a complaint form which you may
provide to citizens who bring these matters to your attention.
The telephone number for our Enforcement Division is
(916) 322-6441.

____________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

-2-

Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any
questions concerning this letter.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[illegible signature]

By: Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

October 15, 1987


Debbie Ortega
Elections Division
Tuolumne County Administration Center
2 South Green Street
Sonora, CA  95370

Re:  87-257

Dear Ms. Ortega:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on October 15, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

RECEIVED FEB - 4 1987

FORM 730
Statement of Economic Interests
For Designated Employees
PUBLIC DOCUMENT

Important Notice to Filers: The Political Reform Act is intended to prevent conflicts of
interest by requiring public officials such as yourself to disclose financial interests which
could foreseeably cause conflicts. In addition, as a public official, you may be required to
disqualify yourself from making, participating in, or attempting to influence any
governmental decision which will affect your financial interests, including those you are
required to report on this Statement. The Fair Political Practices Commission's Guide to the
Political Reform Act: California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials explains what a
conflict of interest is, and when disqualification is required by law. Failure to file your
Statement before the filing deadline may result in penalties including but not limited to late
fines. This statement is a public document open for inspection and reproduction by any
person.

Please type or print in ink

NAME [signature] BIVD-SUIEE

STATE DEPARTMENT AND UNIT OR LOCAL AGENCY | POSITION
U.S.GEOLOGICAL REGIONAL WATER DISTRICT | DIRECTOR

MAILING ADDRESS | TELEPHONE NUMBER
2333 74 6OLO SPAIN DE CAMDIA CA 95310 | (209) 332-2332

Check the appropriate box(es):

☐ INITIAL STATEMENT: The Conflict of Interest Code for your agency became effective on _____(DATE)_____. You
must file a Statement within thirty days after this date disclosing your reportable interests held on this date. You
are not required to disclose income, gifts or loans on this Statement.

☐ ASSUMING OFFICE STATEMENT: You have assumed office on ____________(DATE)____________. You must disclose all
reportable interests, other than income, gifts and loans, held on the date you assumed your position.

☐ Position subject to State Senate confirmation — file thirty days after appointment or nomination.

☐ All other positions — file thirty days after assuming office.

☒ ANNUAL STATEMENT: You are required to file a Statement no later than [signature] 2/1/(DATE)_____ disclosing all
reportable interests held or received during the period from January 1, _____ through December 31, _____.

☐ LEAVING OFFICE STATEMENT: You are leaving or have left your position on ____________(DATE)____________ and must file a
Statement within thirty days of that date. You must disclose all reportable interests held or received during the
period from January 1, ____________ through the date you left office.

☐ CANDIDATE STATEMENT: You are a candidate for elective office. You must disclose all reportable interests,
other than income, gifts and loans, held on the date you filed nomination papers. You must file this Statement no
later than the final filing date of a declaration of candidacy.

LAURENCE G. LEE
CLERK & AUDITOR-CONTROLLER
CLERK/ELECTIONS DIV.

RESOLUTION No. 523

ADOPTING CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR
TUOLUMNE COUNTY WATER DISTRICT NO. 2

BE IT RESOLVED BY THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS OF TUOLUMNE COUNTY WATER
DISTRICT No. 2, as follows:

Section 1. The terms of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18730 (attached hereto)
and any amendments to it shall be incorporated into this Conflict of Interest
Code by reference.

Section 2. The Secretary shall forward a copy hereof to the Board of
Supervisors of Tuolumne County, for approval pursuant to Section 87303
of the Government Code.

Section 3. This code shall be effective when approved by the Board
of Supervisors of Tuolumne County. Said Board of Supervisors is requested to
not adopt this code until at least 30 days after the same is transmitted
to said Board of Supervisors and provide to this Board of Directors reasonable
advance notice of the Board of Supervisors' scheduled approval of said Code.

Section 4. Pursuant to this Conflict of Interest Code, all designated
employees set forth on the Appendix A, attached thereto shall, within
30 days after this Code becomes effective, file statements of economic interests
with the District Secretary, who shall make and retain a copy and forward
the original to the County Clerk of Tuolumne County, who shall be the filing
officer. Such statements shall disclose the kinds of financial interests which
are set forth in the attached Appendix A.

PASSED AND ADOPTED THIS ___30th_____ day of ___June_______, 1980,
by the Board of Directors of Tuolumne County Water District No. 2, by the
following vote:

AYES: Lucille McKee, Bonnie Perry, and Neil Burckart

NOES: John Wise and Peter Kerns

ABSENT:

_____________________
President

ATTEST:

_____________________
Secretary/Acting

APPENDIX A

DESIGNATED EMPLOYEES AND REPORTABLE INVESTMENTS, INCOME
AND INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY

A. The District Directors, the General Manager, and the Auditor/Controller
shall report investments in business entities and sources of income which
do business in, or within one mile of, any service area of the District,
or within one mile of any Regional Sewer Interceptor.

1. Irrigation equipment and supplies, or pipes, valves, fittings,
pumps, meters, etc.
2. Chlorine, alum, lime, copper, sulphate, etc., supplies
3. Water Quality Testing
4. Well drilling equipments and supplies
5 Communications equipment
6. Vehicle and equipment rental
7. Equipment or vehicle repair
8. Office equipment and supplies
9. Construction or building materials
10. Engineering or surveying firms and services
11. Insurance companies or brokerages, including title
insurance companies
12. Accounting or auditing firms or services
13. Banks
14. Real estate selling, development or appraisals
15. Public utilities providing water or sewer service

Said officials shall also report any interests in real estate in or within
one mile of any Regional Sewer Interceptor (but not including the
filer's principal place of residence)

1 The District's service areas are City of Sonora, Columbia, Pinecrest,
Brentwood and Goldmont Forest, Confidence Ridge, Lakewood, Peaceful
Pines, Cuesta Serana, Forest Vista and Phoenix Lake Park.

Mr. Boyd-Snee has not authorized your request; therefore, we are unable to provide specific guidance to you about Mr. Boyd-Snee's duties under the Political Reform Act.1/ (See Regulation 18329(b)(8), copy enclosed.) However, to assist you with these questions we have enclosed a pamphlet which summarizes the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. Please inform Mr. Boyd-Snee that he or his authorized representative may request advice from this office concerning his ability to participate in any pending governmental decisions. The local county counsel or the legal counsel for the water or sanitary districts also could assist Mr. Boyd-Snee with these questions. Complaints alleging that Mr. Boyd-Snee's past conduct has violated the Act should be referred to our Enforcement Division. Enclosed is a copy of a complaint form which you may provide to citizens who bring these matters to your attention. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O.
